title: Athyrium polyanthes
taxonomy: Athyrium polyanthes is classified as a species of fern within the family Athyriaceae. This genus is commonly known as the lady ferns or marsh ferns. The Athyrium polyanthes specifically is found in various parts of the world, including North America. It is often associated with wet environments, such as marshes and stream banks. Within the classification, Athyrium polyanthes is also known by other names, including lady fern and marsh fern.
uses: Athyrium polyanthes, also known as the many-flowered lady fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to be beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, Athyrium polyanthes is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves are also used in herbal teas and infusions to promote digestion and relieve digestive issues. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Athyrium polyanthes for medicinal use.
